headline: Advances in Quantum Simulation, Codes, and Dynamics
summary: Today's research highlights key breakthroughs in quantum physics and quantum computing. Researchers have developed faster tensor-network contraction methods, designed practical error-correcting codes, and proposed novel ways to drive superconductivity and analyze quantum many-body scars.

themes.title: Quantum Computing and Error Correction
themes.body: New developments in quantum information theory introduce highly practical CSS quantum codes with bounded stabilizer weights and establish exact parameter-shift rules for calculating gradients in lossy boson sampling experiments.

themes.title: Many-Body Dynamics and Simulation
themes.body: Innovative approaches to simulating and driving quantum systems emerge, featuring a 100x faster tensor-network contraction algorithm for 2D systems and a theoretical protocol to stabilize superconductivity in moiré heterostructures using dissipation.

themes.title: Quantum Information and Scars
themes.body: Theoretical studies deepen our understanding of quantum states, mapping quantum many-body scars to periodic orbits in spin chains and proving strict superadditivity in entanglement-assisted communication channels.

connections.body: Both papers address classical simulation limits of quantum systems: one accelerates 2D tensor-network contractions (iPEPS) on GPUs, while the other defines the mathematical boundaries of gradient calculations in boson sampling.
